question.text: In case of change in the Investment Manager of an InvIT, within what time period must the Trustee appoint a new Investment Manager from the date of termination?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, in case of any change in investment manager due to removal or otherwise, prior to such change the trustee shall obtain approval from unit holders, and the trustee shall appoint the new investment manager within 3 months from the date of termination of the earlier investment management agreement. **The Trap:** 30 days is the reporting timeline for quarterly reports, not the appointment timeline. The 3-month period applies to both Investment Manager and Project Manager replacements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The same 3-month replacement timeline applies for both Investment Manager and Project Manager changes, ensuring continuity of operations.
